nativeRange.habitat: Tropical and subtropical Asian wetlands, marshes, and shallow stream/river margins; commonly emersed along seasonal waterways

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich substrate or supplementary root tabs
background placement with vertical room to grow
medium to high lighting for compact form and bluish tint

imageCandidates.notes: Submersed studio shot of a cultivated stem; clearly shows the light-green, narrow lanceolate (willow-like) foliage that defines the species. Best single image for ID purposes.
Emersed-form photograph from the Flowgrow plant database; useful as a secondary reference showing the willow-like leaf shape above water.

summary: An easy, fast-growing Asian background stem plant with narrow willow-like leaves; sold in the hobby as 'Hygro Blue' or 'Willow Hygro' for the cool blue-green cast its foliage develops under brighter light, and forgiving across a wide range of freshwater parameters.
careNotes: Hygrophila salicifolia (synonym Ruellia salicifolia Vahl) is a willow-leaved stem plant from tropical and subtropical Asian wetlands (South, Southeast, and East Asia) in the family Acanthaceae. It is widely sold as 'Hygro Blue', 'Blue Hygro', 'Willow Hygro' or 'Willow-leaf Hygrophila', and is regularly confused with Hygrophila angustifolia in the trade because both species share narrow, lanceolate foliage and similar care. Sources agree on a wide tolerance envelope: temperature roughly 18-30 C (optimum 22-28 C), pH 5-8, GH up to ~30 dGH, and KH 2-18 dKH — making it one of the most parameter-flexible aquarium stem plants. Adult submersed height is commonly 30-50 cm with leaves around 11-12 cm long and ~2 cm wide; emersed plants can exceed 1 m and produce small purple flowers. It is consistently rated 'easy' / beginner-friendly and grows under low to medium light without CO2, though medium-to-high light, CO2 (~10-40 mg/L), and steady macro + iron/micronutrient fertilization produce denser stems, faster growth, and the cool blue-green to reddish-tipped coloration the trade name refers to (some retailers also describe distinctly angular, almost triangular leaf shape under strong light). Best used as a background or upper-midground stem; trim regularly to encourage bushy lateral growth, as untrimmed stems tend to grow leggy and shade out lower leaves. A nutrient-rich substrate or root tabs are recommended but not essential — the plant draws heavily from both water column and substrate.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings: snip the top 5-10 cm of a healthy stem, strip the lowest pair of leaves, and replant directly in the substrate. The cut mother stem will branch laterally from the node below the cut, producing a bushier plant. Frequent trimming slows overall vertical growth but thickens the group, which is the recommended approach for a dense background. Emersed plants flower freely (small purple/lavender blooms) and set seed, but seed propagation is essentially unused in the hobby.

primary.name: Aquasabi
primary.notes: Used as the primary source: provided full binomial with author (Vahl) Nees, synonym Ruellia salicifolia Vahl, family Acanthaceae, tropical Asian origin, temperature 18-30 C (optimal 20-28 C), pH 5-8, GH 0-30 dGH, KH 2-18 dKH, medium light, optional CO2, recommended height 30-40 cm, leaf dimensions ~11.5 x 1.9 cm, cuttings propagation, easy care level, emersed flowering, and reddish new growth. Selected over Modern Aquarium and Buce Plant because it carries the most complete taxonomic and parameter data.

additional.name: Flowgrow
additional.notes: Cross-checked synonym (Ruellia salicifolia Vahl), family (Acanthaceae), distribution (South/Southeast/East Asia), temperature 18-30 C (optimum 24-28 C), pH 5-8, KH 2-18 dKH, medium-to-high lighting, CO2 10-40 mg/L, NO3/PO4/K/Fe ranges, background/midground/Dutch-street placement, cutting propagation, easy care, and willow-like leaf morphology with emersed growth confirmed.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Used for trade name 'Hygro Blue' and the description of the bluish-green tint and angular/triangular leaf appearance under good light. Also confirmed temperature 70-82 F (~21-28 C), moderate lighting baseline (looks best moderate-to-high), CO2 not required but beneficial, fast growth rate, background placement, propagation by trimming-and-replanting, and care level 'easy'.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Hygrophila (plant)
additional.notes: Used to confirm genus taxonomy: family Acanthaceae, order Lamiales, with the genus distributed across the tropical and subtropical world. (Note: H. salicifolia itself is not listed on Wikipedia's accepted-species table, but it is widely treated as accepted in the horticultural literature and by Aquasabi/Flowgrow with author citation '(Vahl) Nees', and is documented as distinct from H. angustifolia despite frequent trade-name overlap.)
